source.title: The free-energy principle: a unified brain theory?

confidence_rationale: Foundational FEP review establishing the unified-action-perception-learning framework that grounds the structured-exploration arm. Loaded venue (NRN) and high-impact theoretical contribution. Confidence not higher because FEP-as-empirical-substrate is contested in the literature -- the paper makes a unified-theory claim that is not itself directly testable; downstream empirical predictions are testable but mostly via active-inference instantiations (cf. Friston 2015 PMID 25689102).
failure_signatures: If FEP is fundamentally wrong about the substrate of exploration (epistemic-value-as-free-energy is not a real biological computation), the structured-exploration arm of ARC-065 loses its primary theoretical grounding; only the noise arm (Faisal 2008) and pragmatic curiosity instantiations (Schmidhuber/Pathak) remain.

REE substrates that make precision-minimisation a load-bearing computation (MECH-269b V_s rollout gating, SD-032b dACC adaptive control with PE-precision modulation) implicitly endorse the FEP framework; if FEP is wrong they are at risk too.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The free-energy principle proposes that biological systems minimise the surprise (or equivalently, the variational free energy) of their sensory states. This unifies action, perception, and learning under a single optimisation framework. Exploration is reinterpreted as minimisation of expected free energy, which has both extrinsic-value (reward-seeking) and epistemic-value (uncertainty-reducing) components.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 MECH-314 curiosity-bonus channel has a principled grounding in expected-free-energy minimisation: agents explore to reduce uncertainty about model parameters, not just to maximise reward. The structured-exploration arm of R1 has FEP as its theoretical anchor. REE's existing precision-modulation substrates (MECH-269b V_s rollout gating, SD-032b dACC adaptive control PE-precision) are FEP-aligned, so wiring MECH-314 in this framework is internally coherent.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The FEP is a unified theory whose status as an empirical claim is contested. Friston's theoretical commitment is that all brain function reduces to free-energy minimisation; many neuroscientists treat this as too strong. REE's commitment is more local: precision-weighted prediction error is a real computation with substrate consequences (substrate-landed via MECH-269b et al.). The exploration-as-epistemic-value reading is well-defined in FEP and has computational instantiations (see Friston 2015 PMID 25689102) but is not yet directly tested in REE.
mapping.source_context: Nature Reviews Neuroscience review by Friston (UCL Wellcome Centre for Neuroimaging); cited >5000 times; foundational FEP synthesis.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational venue. Mapping fidelity moderate because FEP is a unified theoretical framework whose empirical substrate-level commitments require additional papers to instantiate (Friston 2015 active inference + epistemic value provides the operational instantiation -- see paired entry).
